It invites readers to rethink the intersection of intimacy, commerce, and agency, urging a nuanced conversation about how digital platforms reshape labor, identity, and societal norms in a post‑pandemic world.
summary_zh: 文章以OnlyFans为镜，探讨数字平台如何重塑劳动、身份与社会规范。它揭示了从早期摄像女郎到2026年多元化创作者的演变，挑战“中彩票”式的成功神话，并指出平台在性工作去污名化中的作用。通过案例、调查与文化现象，作者呼吁读者重新审视亲密、商业与自主的交织，推动关于数字经济与社会变迁的深度对话。
best_quote_en: "Content creation is neither the winning lottery ticket portrayed by the tabloids nor the labor trafficking scam portrayed by antiporn groups," the 2025 survey concluded.
best_quote_zh: "内容创作既不是媒体所描绘的中奖彩票，也不是反色情团体所描述的劳工贩运骗局。"

title: Solo Travel Cities/Foreign Taxes/Canada vs. Nomads
title_zh: 独自旅行城市/外国税务/加拿大与数字游牧者
summary_en: This concise newsletter slices the nomad experience into four sharp insights. It starts by spotlighting the most popular solo‑travel cities—Toronto, Kuala Lumpur, Singapore, São Paulo, and even a tiny Salvadorian town—revealing how free walking tours can serve as social hubs for lone wanderers. The second bite demystifies a common expatriate headache: local income tax liabilities. It catalogs jurisdictions where foreign‑earned income is tax‑neutral, highlighting Latin America’s generosity and the Philippines’ favorable stance for remote workers. The third section warns of Canada’s tightened rules, demanding proof that digital nomads earn entirely outside Canada, a regulatory hurdle that could derail spontaneous work‑travel plans. Finally, the piece pivots to energy policy, noting that U.S. solar installations have eclipsed coal, a trend amplified by Trump‑winning states’ capacity additions. Together, these snippets paint a picture of a world where travel, taxation, immigration, and sustainability intersect, offering travelers actionable data, legal foresight, and a glimpse of shifting global priorities.
summary_zh: 本周简讯以四条要点勾勒数字游牧者的全景：首推热门独行城市，展示免费徒步如何成为社交平台；解析哪些国家对境外收入免税，帮助远程工作者规避税务陷阱；警示加拿大新规要求证明全部收入来自境外，提醒行前准备；并指出美国太阳能已超越煤炭，映射能源转型趋势。内容兼具实用与前瞻，激发读者对旅行、税务、移民与可持续发展的深度思考。
best_quote_en: digital nomads now “must provide sufficient documentation to demonstrate that their income is earned entirely outside Canada and that they will be working remotely for a foreign employer or, if self‑employed, that they will be providing services exclusively to clients outside Canada.”
best_quote_zh: 数字游牧者现在“必须提供足够的文件，证明他们的收入完全来自加拿大境外，并且他们将为外国雇主远程工作，或者如果是自雇，则必须仅为加拿大境外的客户提供服务。”

title: How Not to Die
title_zh: 如何不死亡
summary_en: Y Combinator’s founder delivers a candid talk on startup survival, framing failure as the inevitable fate for half the companies he funds. He argues that the real success metric is simply not dying, and that staying in regular contact, maintaining momentum, and avoiding distractions are the most reliable predictors of longevity. The speech blends hard data—such as the 100 % accuracy of silence as a death signal—with psychological insights about fear of failure, public humiliation, and the power of a committed core user base. He warns against the common “we’ll keep working” mindset that masks resignation, and urges founders to treat their venture as a full‑time, high‑stakes endeavor. The message is both a sobering reminder of startup volatility and a pragmatic playbook: keep iterating, stay engaged, and never give up. It invites founders to view survival as the first step toward wealth, reframing the startup journey as a battle against demoralization rather than a lottery.
summary_zh: YC创始人以坦诚的语调阐述创业生存之道：失败是公司的一半命运，真正的成功是活下来。通过数据和心理洞察，他指出保持定期沟通、持续进展、避免分心是预防死亡的关键。警示“我们会继续努力”往往掩盖放弃，强调将创业视为全职高风险任务。信息既是对创业波动的严肃提醒，也是实用手册：持续迭代、保持投入、永不放弃，重塑生存为战胜沮丧的过程，最终走向财富。
best_quote_en: If you can just avoid dying, you get rich.
best_quote_zh: 只要避免失败，你就能致富。

title: Works in Progress / 100 greatest bird names / Cat water fountain
title_zh: Works in Progress / 100 greatest bird names / Cat water fountain
summary_en: This eclectic newsletter blends a referral‑based incentive scheme with a curated roundup of niche products and thought‑provoking content. The author rewards readers for sharing the free newsletter—offering exclusive PDFs, AMA invites, and personalized doodles—thereby turning passive consumption into a community‑building activity. The piece spotlights "Works in Progress," a Stripe‑backed print magazine that champions underrated ideas across tech, science, and culture, positioning itself as a tangible counter‑point to the digital‑only Substack landscape. The author also shares a whimsical list of the 100 greatest bird names, a stainless‑steel cat water fountain review, and a nostalgic look at early web folk art, all underscored by a personal touch that invites readers to appreciate both tangible and intangible value. The content’s core insight is the power of tangible, well‑designed media to foster deeper engagement, while the referral program demonstrates how value‑based incentives can grow an audience organically. Overall, the newsletter exemplifies a hybrid model of content curation, community building, and product advocacy that resonates with readers seeking both novelty and actionable recommendations.
summary_zh: 这份多元化的简报将推荐奖励与精选产品、前沿想法相结合。作者通过提供独家PDF、AMA邀请和手绘感谢卡，鼓励读者分享免费简报，形成社区成长。重点介绍Stripe支持的印刷杂志《Works in Progress》，强调纸质媒介的独特价值；同时分享百鸟名称、猫用水喷泉、早期网页民俗艺术等趣味内容，体现了对实物与数字体验的双重关注。
best_quote_en: Works in Progress is the only magazine I get delivered on paper; I enjoy reading its designed pages, and getting the extra bits you don’t get online.
best_quote_zh: 《Works in Progress》是我唯一收到纸质版的杂志；我喜欢阅读它精心设计的页面，并获得线上没有的额外内容。

title: HYBE’s Big Hit cracks down on ‘egregious and unlawful conduct’ targeting CORTIS, as fans attach GPS trackers to artists’ vehicles
title_zh: HYBE旗下BIGHIT音乐严厉打击针对CORTIS的‘严重且违法行为’，粉丝在艺人车辆上安装GPS追踪器
summary_en: HYBE’s BIGHIT MUSIC has taken a hard‑line stance against a wave of privacy violations targeting its rookie boy group, CORTIS. The label filed criminal complaints over online abuse, illegal sale of flight data, and the mounting trend of fans affixing GPS trackers to the artists’ vehicles during a Paris tour. By publicly warning fans on its Weverse platform, BIGHIT not only signals zero‑tolerance for “egregious and unlawful conduct” but also underscores the growing intersection of fandom culture and personal data protection. The company’s action—reporting to police, issuing verbal warnings, and deleting unauthorized content—highlights a proactive, multi‑channel response that blends legal recourse with community education. For industry observers, the case illustrates how K‑pop conglomerates are redefining artist safety protocols in an era of hyper‑connected fandoms. It also raises questions about the balance between fan engagement and privacy, prompting a broader dialogue on ethical boundaries in pop culture. Ultimately, HYBE’s measures set a precedent that may compel other labels to adopt stricter safeguards, reshaping the business model of fan‑artist interactions.
summary_zh: HYBE旗下BIGHIT音乐对针对CORTIS的隐私侵犯采取零容忍态度，公开投诉网络辱骂、非法售卖航班信息，并警告粉丝在巴黎巡演期间给艺人车辆装置GPS追踪器。此举不仅凸显了粉丝文化与个人数据保护的交叉点，也展示了企业通过法律、警告与内容删除三管齐下的主动防御模式。对业内而言，此案例揭示了K‑pop巨头正重新定义艺人安全协议，促使标签在高度互联的粉丝生态中更严谨地设定边界。更重要的是，它引发了关于粉丝参与与隐私权平衡的讨论，推动了流行文化伦理边界的再思考。HYBE的做法或将成为行业新标准，重塑粉丝与艺人互动的商业模式。
best_quote_en: Both the sale and purchase of artists’ flight information constitute violation of the Personal Information Protection Act, the Information and Communications Network Act, and other applicable laws.
best_quote_zh: 非法创建和传播针对艺人的有害内容尤其严重，尤其是许多成员仍是未成年人。

title: Agency is not a natural kind (and why that might matter for alignment)
title_zh: 代理不是自然范畴（这对对齐为何重要）
summary_en: This essay interrogates the notion of agency as a natural kind, arguing that it is merely a human‑constructed lens for making sense of complex behaviour. By critiquing the common reduction of agents to goal‑directed utility maximisers, the author exposes the teleological baggage that often accompanies discussions of both human and artificial agency. He suggests that agency should be viewed as an emergent, multi‑layered phenomenon built from boundary maintenance, self‑modelling, and information processing, rather than a discrete, idealised entity. This reframing has concrete implications for AI safety: if we model agents more faithfully, we may better anticipate and mitigate misaligned behaviour. The piece blends philosophical depth with practical insight, offering a nuanced critique of prevailing alignment arguments while opening new avenues for safer AI design. It is especially relevant for researchers at the intersection of AI ethics, cognitive modelling, and safety engineering.
summary_zh: 文章以哲学视角审视‘代理’概念，指出它并非自然范畴，而是人类为理解复杂行为所建构的镜头。作者批判将代理等同于理性目标最大化的做法，强调其隐含的目的论与简化假设。通过讨论人类与AI的代理差异，提出更细致、非目的论的框架可能提升安全性与对齐效果。文章兼具理论深度与实践洞察，适合对AI伦理、对齐与认知建模感兴趣的研究者。
best_quote_en: Agency is a construct that we have developed to help make sense of this mess, but it is only a lens through which we view the world.
best_quote_zh: 代理是我们用来理解这混乱的构造，但它只是我们看世界的镜头。

title: Movement Practice to Strengthen Your Mind-Body Connection | Ido Portal
title_zh: 运动练习强化身心连接 | Ido Portal
summary_en: Ido Portal’s conversation offers a nuanced exploration of how playful movement, distinct from conventional exercise, can transform mental and physical health. He contrasts play with discipline, arguing that the former fosters relaxed awareness while the latter relies on willpower, and demonstrates how this shift rewires default stress responses. By spotlighting transition states—particularly the liminal space between sleep and wakefulness—Portal illustrates how heightened bodily awareness can unlock new insights. The dialogue also introduces micro‑meditation as a practical tool for integrating movement into daily life, and examines the role of friction points in practice as opportunities for growth. The content is deeply actionable, blending expert insight with advanced concepts such as embodied cognition and neuroplasticity. It invites listeners to experiment with movement patterns that cultivate uncertainty, resilience, and a deeper sense of self, offering a compelling blueprint for anyone seeking to strengthen the mind‑body connection.
summary_zh: Ido Portal以玩乐式运动为核心，阐释了它如何在不依赖意志力的前提下重塑压力与恐惧的默认反应。通过探讨睡眠与清醒之间的过渡状态，他展示了身体觉知的提升如何开启新见解。对想要强化身心连接的人而言，这段对话既具可操作性，又兼具前沿洞察，激发我们在日常中以玩乐的方式拥抱不确定性与成长。
best_quote_en: Approaching friction points in your practice with relaxed awareness can rewire your default reactions to stress and fear.
best_quote_zh: 以轻松的觉知方式面对练习中的摩擦点，可以重塑你对压力和恐惧的默认反应。

title:
Most Current Model
Organisms Leaks: 
Perplexity Differencing Often Reveals Finetuning Objectives
title_zh: 最新模型生物泄漏：困惑差分经常揭示微调目标
summary_en: The paper demonstrates that many publicly released model organisms (MOs)—finetuned language models with specific hidden behaviors—exhibit leakage: the finetuned objective surfaces even under unrelated, short prefills. By generating thousands of generic completions and ranking them by perplexity difference against a reference model, the authors can surface top‑ranked outputs that reveal the hidden behavior. The method works across 76 MOs, spanning 0.5–70 B parameters and various families, and is effective even when the reference model is from a different family. In the AuditBench benchmark, providing auditors with these top‑ranked completions boosts detection rates to near‑state‑of‑the‑art. The study highlights two practical implications: (1) MOs intended to model covert or trigger‑gated behaviors should resist leakage to be credible testbeds; (2) perplexity differencing offers a low‑cost first‑pass audit for any finetuned model, though it cannot prove absence of behavior. The work underscores the need for better context gating and suggests that perplexity differencing can serve as a model‑diffing tool beyond MOs. Its simplicity, data‑driven nature, and broad applicability make it a valuable contribution to auditing and understanding finetuned LLMs.
summary_zh: 论文揭示，公开发布的模型生物（MOs）在无关短前缀下往往泄露其微调目标。作者通过生成成千上万个通用完成并按与参考模型的困惑差分排序，能快速提取揭示隐藏行为的高排名输出。该方法在76个不同规模、不同家族的MOs上均有效，即使参考模型来自不同家族亦能检测。将这些高排名完成提供给AuditBench审计者，检测率接近SOTA。研究提出两点实践意义：一、若MOs用于模拟隐蔽或触发门控行为，应避免泄漏以保持可信；二、困惑差分可作为低成本的首轮审计手段，但不能证明行为缺失。其简洁、数据驱动、通用性强，使其成为审计与模型差异化的有力工具。
best_quote_en: We found that many current publicly available model organisms leak: finetuning induces a bias toward expressing instilled behaviors outside their intended context.
best_quote_zh: 我们发现许多当前公开可用的模型生物泄漏：微调导致在其预期上下文之外表达植入行为的偏差。

title: The Sartorialist / Asterix and the Missing Scroll
title_zh: The Sartorialist / Asterix与失踪卷轴
summary_en: The article juxtaposes two seemingly disparate book reviews—Scott Schuman’s street‑fashion compendium *The Sartorialist* and Jean‑Yves Ferri’s latest *Asterix* adventure—to illuminate how narrative framing and visual storytelling shape reader engagement. Schuman’s work is praised for its obsessive focus on individual design choices, turning everyday New Yorkers into style icons and offering a curated archive of contemporary fashion that doubles as a historical snapshot of 2010’s streetwear. In contrast, Ferri and Conrad’s *Asterix and the Missing Scroll* reinvigorates a classic comic by weaving a witty, politically‑charged plot that satirizes WikiLeaks, while the artwork retains Uderzo’s legacy yet pushes the envelope with crisp panels and dynamic humor. Both books exemplify how authenticity—whether through candid photography or playful satire—can elevate genre expectations. The piece invites readers to consider the power of context, the role of humor in cultural critique, and the importance of preserving tradition while innovating, offering actionable insights for creators and enthusiasts alike.
summary_zh: 文章对比两本书评：Scott Schuman的街头时尚集《The Sartorialist》和Jean‑Yves Ferri的新版《阿斯特里克斯与失踪卷轴》。前者以真实街拍捕捉纽约人造型，成为时尚史料；后者以讽刺WikiLeaks的情节与细腻画面重燃经典漫画。两者共同展示叙事与视觉如何塑造读者体验，提醒创作者在继承与创新间寻找平衡。
best_quote_en: "Asterix and the Missing Scroll, the 36th entry in the series, is more inventive in plot and much funnier both in script and visuals than its predecessor."
best_quote_zh: 《阿斯特里克斯与失踪卷轴》是系列第36部作品，在情节上更具创新性，脚本和视觉上也比前作更幽默。

title: Essentials: The Science of Love, Desire & Attachment
title_zh: 必修课：爱情、欲望与依恋的科学
summary_en: Huberman’s episode dissects the neurobiology and psychology that underlie desire, love, and attachment, offering a rare blend of academic rigor and practical guidance. He maps childhood attachment styles—secure, anxious, avoidant, and disorganized—to adult romantic dynamics, illustrating how early caregiver interactions imprint on neural circuits that govern trust, intimacy, and conflict resolution. The discussion extends to the autonomic nervous system, explaining how physiological arousal and sympathetic‑parasympathetic balance shape relational synchrony, a concept he terms the “seesaw analogy.” Huberman then translates these insights into actionable tools: self‑assessment of attachment style, real‑time autonomic monitoring, and evidence‑based supplement strategies (e.g., maca root, tongkat ali, tribulus) to boost libido. By weaving neurochemical pathways (dopamine, oxytocin, testosterone) with behavioral patterns, he provides listeners with a holistic framework to diagnose and repair relationship dysfunctions. The episode is a compelling resource for psychologists, couples therapists, and anyone eager to fuse science with intimacy practice, highlighting the power of self‑awareness and physiological regulation in cultivating lasting bonds.
summary_zh: Huberman在此集中探讨欲望、爱情与依恋的心理与生物学基础，结合儿童依恋类型与成年恋爱模式的映射，阐释神经回路与自主神经系统如何影响情感同步。他提供可操作工具：自我评估依恋类型、实时自主监测及基于证据的补充剂（麦芽根、东革阿里、石蒜）提升性欲。通过神经递质与行为模式的交织，帮助读者诊断并修复关系功能障碍，强调自我觉察与生理调节在建立持久关系中的关键作用。
best_quote_en: Childhood attachment styles can shape adult romantic relationships.
best_quote_zh: 童年依恋风格会影响成年人的浪漫关系。

title: Merlin appoints former Downtown exec Harmen Hemminga as VP, Business Development
title_zh: Merlin任命前Downtown高管Harmen Hemminga为业务发展副总裁
summary_en: Merlin’s appointment of Harmen Hemminga, a seasoned veteran of Downtown Music and its distribution arm FUGA, signals a strategic pivot toward collective value creation and emerging technologies within the independent music sector. Hemminga’s deep experience in product strategy, licensing, and partnerships positions him to spearhead AI‑driven licensing initiatives—an area Merlin has been actively expanding, evidenced by recent deals with ElevenLabs and Udio. The move underscores Merlin’s intent to strengthen its global membership base, now spanning 70+ countries, and to enhance its competitive edge against major labels by leveraging innovative tech solutions. For industry observers, the appointment highlights the increasing convergence of traditional music business expertise with AI and data‑driven platforms, offering a blueprint for how independent collectives can scale sustainably while safeguarding artist rights. The leadership change also reflects a broader trend of independent music entities investing in strategic talent to navigate a rapidly evolving digital marketplace.
summary_zh: Merlin任命前Downtown高管Harmen Hemminga为业务发展副总裁，凸显其在独立音乐领域推动AI与新技术合作的战略。Hemminga在产品策略、许可与合作伙伴关系方面的深厚经验，使Merlin能进一步深化与AI公司的许可协议，并为全球70余国会员创造更大价值。此举不仅强化了Merlin在数字音乐市场的竞争力，也为独立音乐企业如何通过技术创新实现可持续增长提供了范例。
best_quote_en: "His experience both with labels and in distribution gives him invaluable perspective on the issues and opportunities our members face."
best_quote_zh: "他在标签和发行方面的经验为我们成员面临的问题和机遇提供了宝贵视角。"

title: Jess Cartner-Morley on fashion: still wearing stripes? It’s time to join the dots
title_zh: Jess Cartner‑Morley谈时尚：仍穿条纹？是时候把点连起来了
summary_en: Jess Cartner‑Morley’s latest Guardian column revisits the age‑old rivalry between stripes and spots, framing it as a cultural pendulum swing rather than a mere aesthetic choice. She traces stripes’ evolution from the utilitarian sailor’s stripe to the corporate staple that signals authority, while mapping spots’ journey from plague‑laden symbols to the playful, feminine icon embraced by celebrities and street style alike. By juxtaposing historical anecdotes with contemporary runway examples, Cartner‑Morley argues that the resurgence of polka‑dots reflects a broader shift away from rigid professionalism toward individuality and “cuteness” as an aspirational aesthetic. The piece is rich in expert commentary—quoting Yayoi Kusama, Damien Hirst, and Roy Lichtenstein—providing both a narrative and a data‑driven lens on fashion’s cyclical nature. Readers gain a nuanced understanding of how visual motifs carry social meaning, how they are repurposed by different industries, and why the dot may be the next mainstream statement for a generation tired of monochrome conformity.
summary_zh: Jess Cartner‑Morley在《卫报》专栏里把条纹与波点的对立重新定义为文化摆动，探讨从海军到企业的条纹与从瘟疫到明星的波点如何重塑时尚语义。她指出，波点的复兴体现了人们对个性化与“可爱”审美的渴求，正是对单调职场形象的反叛。
best_quote_en: “A polka‑dot has the form of the sun, which is a symbol of the energy of the whole world and our living life, and also the form of the moon, which is calm.”
best_quote_zh: “波点的形状像太阳，象征着整个世界的能量和我们的生命，也像月亮，象征平静。”

title: Holding a Program in One's Head
title_zh: 将程序放在脑海中
summary_en: The essay argues that true mastery of programming hinges on an architect’s ability to internalize an entire codebase, much like a mathematician visualizes a problem space. It critiques conventional corporate workflows that fragment work, introduce distractions, and dilute ownership, thereby preventing the mental model required for rapid iteration and deep understanding. Practical prescriptions—long uninterrupted sessions, succinct languages, bottom‑up design, frequent rewrites, and minimal multi‑author edits—serve as a playbook for cultivating a holistic mental model. The piece also reflects on organizational culture, suggesting that the very structure of large firms stifles the individual genius that drives innovation. By framing programming as a cognitive exercise, the author invites both solo developers and teams to re‑evaluate their habits, offering a roadmap to reclaim the creative agency that large enterprises often suppress.
summary_zh: 文章把写代码比作数学家把问题空间放进脑中，强调完整的程序认知是高效迭代的前提。它批判企业的分散流程、干扰和多作者编辑，导致程序难以“装进脑子”。作者给出长时间专注、简洁语言、逐层设计、频繁重写、少量多作者等实践建议，帮助开发者恢复对代码的整体把控。文章还探讨了组织文化对个人创造力的压制，呼吁团队重新审视工作习惯，重新获得在大公司中被压抑的独立思考与创新能力。
best_quote_en: You hold the whole program in your head, and you can manipulate it at will.
best_quote_zh: 你把整个程序放在脑海里，你可以随意操作它。

title: What’s in my NOW? — Todd Henion
title_zh: 我的当下有什么？——托德·海尼恩
summary_en: Todd Henion’s piece is a contemplative inventory of the tools—both physical and digital—that shape his daily life and mindset. He frames the discussion around James Carse’s finite/infinite game dichotomy, suggesting that embracing an infinite outlook reshapes personal goals and responsibilities. Henion then walks readers through a curated list of items: a JetStream pen for smooth writing, a versatile 11‑in‑1 screwdriver that unlocks repair cafés, and a library app that keeps a pocketful of books ready for distraction‑free learning. The digital section highlights Instapaper and the Libby app, underscoring how curated reading can transform idle scrolling into intentional engagement. Interwoven with these practicalities is a philosophical anchor: Jiddu Krishnamurti’s assertion that “I don’t mind what happens,” which Henion interprets as a release from outcome‑attachment and an invitation to inner freedom. The article invites readers to audit their own “NOW” tools, blending actionable habits with reflective wisdom to cultivate a more intentional, resilient life.
summary_zh: 托德·海尼恩用一份个人工具清单，探讨了物理、数字与精神三大维度的生活方式。先以“有限与无限游戏”理论为框架，强调无限视角如何重塑目标与角色。随后列举JetStream笔、11‑in‑1螺丝刀、Libby与Instapaper等实用工具，展示如何将日常小物转化为提升专注与修复能力的手段。文章核心在于引用Krishnamurti的“我不在乎发生了什么”，将其视为放下结果执着、获得内在自由的关键。整体鼓励读者审视自身的“现在”，将行动与思考结合，构建更有意识、更具韧性的生活。
best_quote_en: I don’t mind what happens. That is the essence of inner freedom. It is a timeless spiritual truth: release attachment to outcomes, deep inside yourself, you’ll feel good no matter what.
best_quote_zh: 我不在乎发生了什么，那是内在自由的本质。它是一条永恒的精神真理：释放对结果的执着，深入内心，你无论发生什么都会感到安好。

title: Essentials: Tools for Hormone Optimization in Males | Dr. Kyle Gillett
title_zh: 必备：男性激素优化工具 | Kyle Gillett 博士
summary_en: In this episode of Huberman Lab Essentials, Dr. Kyle Gillett, a dual‑board‑certified physician in family and obesity medicine, unpacks a science‑based framework for male hormone optimization. He emphasizes that accurate bloodwork is the foundation—without it, any intervention is blind. The discussion weaves together nutrition, caloric restriction, exercise, and stress management as pillars that influence testosterone, DHEA, and overall endocrine health across the lifespan. Gillett also demystifies testosterone replacement therapy (TRT), outlining dosing, monitoring, and side‑effect profiles, and contrasts it with oral agents like clomiphene and SERMs. He reviews evidence‑backed supplements—creatine, L‑carnitine, Tongkat Ali, Fadogia agrestis—highlighting mechanisms, dosages, and safety signals such as TMAO production. The episode culminates in practical guidance on integrating these tools, from choosing the right lab panel to crafting a personalized lifestyle plan. For listeners, the takeaway is a holistic, data‑driven roadmap that balances medical intervention with lifestyle optimization, empowering men to take proactive control of their hormonal health.
summary_zh: 本期 Huberman Lab 讨论了男性激素优化的科学工具，从血液检测到营养、运动，再到激素替代疗法与天然补剂。内容兼具临床数据与实操建议，帮助听众系统评估自身激素状态并制定个性化方案。
best_quote_en: We discuss science‑based tools for optimizing male hormones across the lifespan, including the role of bloodwork, nutrition, and exercise in supporting healthy hormone levels.
best_quote_zh: 我们讨论了基于科学的工具，以在整个生命周期内优化男性激素，包括血液检查、营养和运动在支持健康激素水平中的作用。

title: Talk is of newlywed Taylor Swift taking a break from music. Did I take a nap and wake up in the 1950s? | Laura Snapes
title_zh: 新婚泰勒·斯威夫特停下音乐的传闻——我是否从1950年代醒来？ | Laura Snapes
summary_en: In a sharp, culturally resonant essay, Laura Snapes turns Taylor Swift’s high‑profile marriage into a lens for interrogating the myth that happiness inevitably dulls ambition. She critiques the media’s tendency to package celebrity unions as a quieting of creative fire, juxtaposing Swift’s relentless output with the trope of the ‘housewife’ artist. The piece draws on historical parallels—from 1950s domesticity to Beyoncé’s marital albums—to argue that the narrative of marriage as a creative pause is both outdated and reductive. Snapes challenges readers to reconsider the binary of personal joy versus professional drive, and to recognize that public expectations can pressure artists into self‑censorship. Ultimately, the essay calls for a more nuanced understanding of how marriage and artistry can coexist, urging a shift away from the “happiness equals boredom” cliché.
summary_zh: 文章以泰勒·斯威夫特婚姻为切入点，批判传统观念将婚后生活视为单调停滞的刻板印象，呼吁多元化的艺术与生活叙事。作者探讨明星婚姻如何被媒体包装，质疑“幸福即无聊”这一陈词滥调，并以斯威夫特为例，讨论公众对女性艺术家在婚后是否继续创作的期望，最终指出个人选择与社会期待的张力。
best_quote_en: The idea that happiness makes for boring work is rooted in tired tortured‑artist tropes, as if there aren’t millions of great pieces of art about domesticity’s many joys – and sorrows.
best_quote_zh: 幸福让工作变得乏味的想法根植于疲惫的受折磨艺术家陋习，仿佛没有数以百万计关于日常生活喜怒哀乐的杰出作品。

title: Fragile Correctness: Cases of reasoning harming performance
title_zh: 脆弱正确性：推理导致性能受损的案例
summary_en: This post dissects a counterintuitive phenomenon in large language models—‘fragile correctness’—where a model momentarily locks onto a correct answer only to abandon it after further reasoning. By tracking the chain‑of‑thought trajectories on MMLU‑pro and GPQA‑diamond, the author quantifies an alarming 14.9% answer‑loss rate, with 4.3% of cases exhibiting high confidence loss. The work introduces a probing framework that flags the fragile state and tests four linear classifiers (future_loss, future_change_to_wrong, future_answer_flip, correctness). Although the answer‑flip detector yields the highest raw accuracy, the future_loss probe offers the most practical gain, boosting accuracy by 5.2% on loss cases. The study highlights the limitations of current probes, the scarcity of clean training data, and the need for broader model coverage. It invites researchers to validate the fragile correctness hypothesis across architectures, refine detection, and explore interventions that preserve correct answers—an essential step toward more reliable, transparent reasoning in AI systems.
summary_zh: 作者揭示大型语言模型在推理过程中出现的‘脆弱正确性’现象：模型先得出正确答案，却因进一步推理而失误。通过对MMLU‑pro与GPQA‑diamond的链式推理轨迹进行量化，发现14.9%的答案失误率，其中4.3%高置信度失误。作者提出四种线性探测器，虽然答案翻转探测器准确率最高，但专门检测脆弱状态的future_loss探测器在干预时提升5.2%准确率。研究指出探测器受限于样本稀缺，呼吁在更多模型上验证假设并改进干预策略，提升推理可靠性。
best_quote_en: There is evidence to suggest that models sometimes enter a ‘fragile correctness’ state where the model obtains the correct answer but further reasoning leads to it getting the answer wrong.
best_quote_zh: 有证据表明模型有时会进入‘脆弱正确性’状态，即模型先得到正确答案，但进一步推理导致答案错误。

title: Donald Trump Celebrates America’s Two-Hundred-and-Fiftieth Birthday
title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普庆祝美国二百五十周年
summary_en: The article offers a vivid, satirical chronicle of Donald Trump’s grandiose celebration of America’s 250th anniversary, juxtaposing his self‑styled “America First” narrative against the historic backdrop of Washington, D.C., and the Theodore Roosevelt Presidential Library. By weaving eyewitness observations—cranes over the National Mall, the controversial Freedom 250 branding, and the politicized state fair—into a broader critique of partisan appropriation of national heritage, the piece invites readers to interrogate the blurring of public commemoration and personal propaganda. It highlights Trump’s strategic use of symbols, from the “America Is Back” banners to the faux “U.F.C.” fights, to galvanize a specific audience while alienating others. The narrative also touches on the logistical mishaps of the fair and the media’s reaction, underscoring the performative nature of contemporary political rallies. Ultimately, the article functions as a case study of how political leaders repurpose historical milestones for personal branding, prompting reflection on the integrity of national celebrations in an era of hyper‑polarization.
summary_zh: 文章以辛辣讽刺的笔触记录了特朗普在美国二百五十周年庆典中的盛大表演，展示他如何将“美国优先”理念与华盛顿和罗斯福图书馆的历史背景交织。文中通过现场观察——国家广场的起重机、争议的 Freedom 250 品牌、以及充满党派色彩的州博览会——揭示了公共纪念与个人宣传的模糊界限。文章强调特朗普利用标语、假“U.F.C.”表演等符号拉拢特定受众，却也疏远他人，并点出庆典的物流失误与媒体反应，凸显当代政治秀的表演性。整体而言，它是一份关于政治领袖如何将历史里程碑变为个人品牌的案例研究，促使人们思考在极化时代下国家庆典的真实性与完整性。
best_quote_en: "Do you think people appreciate what a fantastic job we did in building and operating the Great American State Fair at the National Mall, packed with happy people, and everybody loving it? Ask yourself this simple question, ‘DO YOU THINK THAT OBUMA OR SLEEPY JOE BIDEN COULD HAVE DONE IT?’ THE ANSWER IS NO!"
best_quote_zh: "你们认为人们会欣赏我们在国家广场建造和运营伟大美国州博览会的精彩工作吗？问问自己一个简单的问题，‘你认为奥布马或睡懒觉的乔·拜登能做到吗？’答案是不会！"

title: Fluidity Forum 2026
title_zh: 流动论坛 2026
summary_en: Fluidity Forum 2026 is a hybrid of community building, meta‑rational discourse, and low‑barrier participation. The event’s core is a participatory house‑party that invites speakers, artists, and volunteers to co‑create a learning environment. By foregrounding openness to diverse perspectives and actively interrogating personal biases, the forum democratizes knowledge production and blurs the line between audience and contributor. The schedule blends solo talks, panels, and informal gaming, reflecting a meta‑rational curriculum that spans developmental theory, internal family systems, and algorithmic critique. The affordability model—shared rooms and a pay‑what‑you‑can option—signals a commitment to inclusivity that counters elitist academic cultures. However, the informal structure may limit scalability and documentation of insights. Future iterations could benefit from systematic recording, online dissemination, and clearer thematic curation to amplify impact while preserving the spontaneous, community‑driven ethos that defines the forum.
summary_zh: 流动论坛 2026 是一个聚焦元理性与社会实践的社区聚会，强调开放思维、低成本参与和跨学科对话。它通过让参与者主动分享演讲、艺术和志愿服务，打破传统受众壁垒，营造一种共建式学习氛围。对我而言，这种模式既是对知识民主化的尝试，也是对自我偏见的自我检视。未来类似活动若能更系统地记录讨论成果并扩展线上传播，将更有助于扩大影响力并推动理性文化的可持续发展。
best_quote_en: The event culture emphasizes being open to a range of perspectives, being aware of your biases and treating them as participants in the event rather than letting them control you.
best_quote_zh: 我们不想让金钱成为我们社区的门槛。

title: Book Freak #265 — Deep Cut Edition
title_zh: 书虫 #265 — 深度剪辑版
summary_en: The piece resurrects H.G. Wells’s forgotten 1928 novel *Mr. Blettsworthy on Rampole Island*, framing it as a micro‑cosm of 20th‑century disillusionment and a precursor to the inner‑space genre. The author unpacks Wells’s dual narrative of a comfortable Englishman’s psychological collapse and a savage island that mirrors civilized hypocrisy. By treating the island’s cannibals and megatherian sloth as allegories for dogmatic thinking and societal rituals, the article invites readers to interrogate the “assurances” we accept as children and the “megatheria”—habits or beliefs—kept alive without justification. The Deep Cut edition’s discussion prompts turn the book into an interactive reflective exercise, encouraging readers to identify personal falsehoods and outdated traditions. The analysis is both literary and sociological, positioning the work as a case study of how fiction can expose the absurdities of our social constructs. It invites a deeper conversation about resilience, authenticity, and the cost of clinging to outdated narratives.
summary_zh: 这篇文章重新挖掘了 H.G. 威尔斯1928年的遗作《Blettsworthy先生与拉姆波岛》，将其视为对20世纪幻灭的微观写照。作者剖析主角的心理崩溃与岛上“野蛮人”与“巨型树懒”所映射的社会假象，邀请读者反思童年时被灌输的“保证”与无意义的“古怪习俗”。通过讨论提问，鼓励读者识别自身的虚假信念与陈旧传统，探讨在失去乐观后仍能坚持生活与爱。文章兼具文学与社会学视角，展示小说如何揭示社会构造的荒诞，并促成关于韧性与真实性的深入对话。
best_quote_en: "The abnormal is only the normal disproportioned."
best_quote_zh: “异常只是失衡的正常。”

title: The Tim Ferriss Show Transcripts: The “Divine Leaf” with 8,000+ Years of Use — Exploring the Many Benefits of Coca with Dr. Andrew Weil and Wade Davis (#871)
title_zh: The Tim Ferriss Show Transcripts: The “Divine Leaf” with 8,000+ Years of Use — Exploring the Many Benefits of Coca with Dr. Andrew Weil and Wade Davis (#871)
summary_en: The episode is a deep dive into coca’s 8,000‑year legacy as a medicinal plant, disentangling myth from science. Dr. Andrew Weil and anthropologist Wade Davis bring ethnobotanical evidence, clinical anecdotes, and policy history to show how coca’s alkaloid profile—beyond cocaine—offers gut regulation, metabolic benefits, and a gentle stimulant that could replace harsher drugs. They critique decades of political demonization rooted in colonial power dynamics, argue for research funding and regulatory reform, and outline practical steps—from consumer awareness to FDA‑approved indications—to rehabilitate coca. The discussion blends personal narrative, cultural context, and actionable research proposals, inviting listeners to re‑evaluate the plant’s role in health, economics, and indigenous rights. It challenges the listener to move from passive curiosity to active advocacy, positioning coca as a case study in how science, policy, and storytelling can correct historical injustices while unlocking therapeutic potential.
summary_zh: 这集节目深入探讨了可可的8000年医学传统，拆解迷思与科学。安德鲁·韦尔医生与人类学家韦德·戴维斯用民族植物学证据、临床轶事和政策历史，展示可可的多种生物碱如何提供肠道调节、代谢益处和温和刺激，可能替代更强效药物。他们批判基于殖民权力的政治妖魔化，呼吁研究资助与法规改革，并列出从公众认知到FDA批准的可行路径。节目将个人叙事、文化语境与可行研究相结合，鼓励听众从被动好奇转向主动倡导，将可可视为纠正历史不公与释放治疗潜能的案例研究。
best_quote_en: ‘The plant was demonized before there was a cocaine problem.’
best_quote_zh: ‘植物在有可卡因问题之前就已被妖魔化。’

title: Bella Figura founder Alexi Cory-Smith dies, aged 58
title_zh: Bella Figura创始人Alexi Cory‑Smith逝世，享年58岁
summary_en: The sudden loss of Alexi Cory‑Smith, co‑founder and CEO of Bella Figura Music, reverberates across the music‑catalog industry and beyond. At 58, she had already carved a remarkable trajectory: a seven‑year stint at BMG where she forged high‑profile relationships, then a rapid rise to SVP and EVP roles before launching Bella Figura in 2022. The startup, now a $160 million‑plus catalog powerhouse, has secured deals with artists such as David Gray and Paul Epworth, signaling a new era of data‑driven, artist‑centric rights management. Cory‑Smith’s story illustrates how visionary leadership, combined with deep industry expertise, can transform a niche market into a competitive juggernaut. Her legacy underscores the critical role of women in shaping the business side of music, and it invites reflection on the sustainability of catalog‑based revenue models in an AI‑driven age. As Bella Figura moves forward, the industry will likely draw lessons from her blend of entrepreneurial agility and strategic partnership building.
summary_zh: Alexi Cory‑Smith的逝世让音乐版权产业感受到深刻震荡。她在BMG的七年经验与与滚石、Roger Waters等巨星的合作，奠定了她在行业内的权威。2022年创立Bella Figura后，公司已投放逾1.6亿美元于音乐版权，签约David Gray、Paul Epworth等艺术家，展示了数据驱动、以艺术家为中心的商业模式。她的故事凸显女性在音乐商业领域的领导力，也为未来AI时代的版权变现模式提供了可借鉴的经验。
best_quote_en: Alexi built Bella Figura Music from the ground up, bringing the vision and passion from a successful career in the music industry.
best_quote_zh: 我们深感悲痛，宣布我们的联合创始人兼CEO Alexi Cory‑Smith的突然与意外离世。

title: Stuff
title_zh: 东西
summary_en: The essay reflects on the paradox of modern abundance: as goods become cheaper and easier to acquire, their perceived value blurs, leading to clutter that drains mental energy and financial resources. The author recounts personal experiences of hoarding from childhood to adulthood, noting that the real value of an item lies not in its price but in its utility. He argues that possessions are illiquid assets; without a plan to use or dispose of them, they become burdens. The piece critiques consumer culture’s psychological tactics—making shopping a leisure activity—and offers pragmatic self‑checks: ask whether a purchase will improve life, be used consistently, or simply serve as a bargain. The author’s shift to a minimalist mindset, especially around non‑essential goods, underscores a broader societal transition where excess has become a hidden weight. The narrative invites readers to reconsider attachment to material items, highlighting the cognitive load of clutter and the opportunity to reclaim freedom by prioritizing services over goods.
summary_zh: 作者以个人经历揭示现代物质泛滥的悖论：物价低廉、获取便捷，却导致物品价值被稀释，堆积成心理与经济负担。他强调物品的真正价值在于使用，而非价格，质疑消费文化把购物变成休闲的手段，并提出自检法：购买前问自己是否能提升生活、持续使用或仅是便宜货。通过简化生活，作者展示了社会从“拥有”到“使用”的转变，提醒读者反思对物品的执着，解放自我。
best_quote_en: The only way you're ever going to extract any value from it is to use it.
best_quote_zh: 真正能从中获得价值的唯一方式就是使用它。

title: The Intimate Legacies of a White-Supremacist Coup
title_zh: 白人至上政变的亲密遗产
summary_en: The article traces the 1898 Wilmington coup through the intimate memories of Cynthia Brown’s family, revealing how a single act of white supremacy rippled through generations. By intertwining personal testimony with archival silence—locked libraries, hidden memoirs, and clandestine memorials—the piece demonstrates the enduring power of collective memory to shape identity and civic agency. Brown’s return to Wilmington and her work in public history illustrate the struggle to confront a suppressed past, while the juxtaposition of official monuments and guerrilla graffiti underscores the contested narratives that still haunt the city. The narrative invites readers to consider how historical erasure perpetuates social inequities, and how reclaiming truth can become a catalyst for healing and political accountability. Ultimately, the story serves as a case study in how the past is never truly buried, but instead woven into the fabric of contemporary democracy, demanding vigilance and active remembrance.
summary_zh: 文章通过辛西亚·布朗家族的亲身回忆，揭示了1898年温斯洛威尔白人至上政变的代际影响。锁闭的档案、隐藏的回忆录与地下纪念碑交织，展示了集体记忆如何塑造身份与公民责任。布朗回归本土、从事公共历史工作，凸显对被压抑过去的挑战；官方纪念碑与街头涂鸦的对比，映射争议叙事。本文提醒读者，历史抹杀助长社会不平等，重拾真相可成为治愈与政治问责的催化剂，说明过去永不被埋没，而是深植当代民主，需警觉与主动记忆。
best_quote_en: The truth would set us free, as the Bible promises.
best_quote_zh: 真相会使我们自由，正如圣经所承诺的。

title: Cable Luggage Lock/Compact Speaker/Duffel Bag in a Pouch
title_zh: 带绳锁/紧凑音箱/可收纳手提袋
summary_en: The article reads like a compact travel toolkit, offering practical, low‑cost solutions that blend security, audio, hygiene, and packing into a single narrative. It begins with a retractable cable lock that turns any suitcase into a tamper‑proof fortress—an inexpensive, universal fix that counters the pervasive fear of theft on trains and public transit. The author then pivots to the JBL Go speaker, praising its surprisingly robust bass and water‑resistance, positioning it as a pocket‑sized audio upgrade for on‑the‑go travelers. A brief but thoughtful detour into electric toothbrushes highlights the trade‑offs between battery‑powered and rechargeable models, underscoring the importance of portability for frequent flyers. The pièce de résistance is the packable duffel bag, which collapses into a pouch and solves the “extra‑carry‑on” problem with elegance. Together, these recommendations form a cohesive, actionable guide that elevates everyday travel, offering readers tangible tools to streamline packing, protect belongings, and enjoy high‑quality sound without breaking the bank.
summary_zh: 文章像一套旅行工具箱，提供低成本、实用的安全、音频、卫生和打包方案。先是可拉伸绳锁，防盗；再是 JBL Go 便携音箱，水防且音质佳；随后是电动牙刷的电池与充电式对比；最后是可折叠手提袋，解决额外行李需求。整体呈现出一套可操作的旅行指南，帮助读者轻松打包、保管行李、享受高品质音效，兼顾实用与经济。
best_quote_en: Ever need to come home with more items than you left with? You could buy additional luggage on the other end, but when I can anticipate this happening, like I did this trip from shopping and getting useful trade show swag, I bring along a duffel bag that packs down into a little pouch.
best_quote_zh: 当你需要把比出门时多的东西带回家时，你可以在另一端购买额外行李，但当我能预见到这种情况时，就像我在购物并收集有用的展会赠品时那样，我会携带一只可折叠手提袋，折叠成小袋装着。

title: How Genes Shape Your Risk Taking & Morals | Dr. Kathryn Paige Harden
title_zh: 基因如何塑造你的冒险行为与道德观 | 哈德博士
summary_en: This episode delves into the nuanced interplay between genetics and upbringing, revealing how specific alleles can bias risk‑taking, moral judgment, and addiction propensity. Dr. Harden highlights sex‑specific genetic effects, showing that male and female genomes differentially influence impulsivity and substance use. The discussion extends beyond individual behavior to societal constructs—how biological predispositions shape collective notions of sin, punishment, and forgiveness. By integrating epigenetic insights and twin‑study data, the conversation underscores that genes do not dictate fate but tilt probability landscapes, offering a richer, data‑driven framework for understanding human conduct. The episode invites listeners to reconsider deterministic narratives, emphasizing the ethical responsibility of acknowledging genetic influence while preserving agency. It serves as a compelling call for interdisciplinary dialogue, urging psychologists, policymakers, and the public to navigate the fine line between nature and nurture with nuance and compassion.
summary_zh: 本期节目揭示基因与成长环境交织，如何微调冒险、道德与成瘾倾向。它让我们重新审视“命定”与“自由”，并提醒我们在政策与日常判断中兼顾生物与文化的双重影响。
best_quote_en: Genes shape your propensity for addiction and impulsivity in males versus females.
best_quote_zh: 基因塑造了男性和女性对成瘾与冲动的倾向。

title: The “Divine Leaf” with 8,000+ Years of Use — Exploring the Many Benefits of Coca with Dr. Andrew Weil and Wade Davis (#871)
title_zh: 《神圣之叶》——8000 年的使用历史：与安德鲁·韦尔博士和韦德·戴维斯探讨可可叶的多重益处（#871）
summary_en: Tim Ferriss’s latest episode pulls back the curtain on the coca leaf, a plant that has sustained Andean cultures for 8,000 years yet has been demonized by Western drug policy. With co‑hosts Dr. Andrew Weil and ethnobotanist Wade Davis, the conversation blends ethnographic lore with hard science: coca’s 14 alkaloids, its proven benefits for altitude sickness, digestive health, and mood, and the stark absence of addiction or toxicity in centuries of use. The hosts dissect the 1950 UN commission that framed coca as a narcotic through fear and colonial bias, and they challenge the modern narrative that equates the leaf with cocaine. They also explore contemporary research that positions coca as a potential ally against opioid addiction and a source of nutraceuticals. Beyond the science, the episode frames coca as a cultural genocide when outlawed, urging listeners to rethink plant‑medicine relationships. It invites a reevaluation of policy, commerce, and the ethics of drug regulation.
summary_zh: 本期节目揭示可可叶——8000 年以来安第斯人民的药用与文化宝藏，却因西方禁毒政策被误解。与安德鲁·韦尔医生、民族植物学家韦德·戴维斯对话，探讨 14 种生物碱、海拔适应、消化与情绪益处，及其无成瘾毒性。深入剖析 1950 年 UN 委员会的殖民偏见与恐惧，挑战将叶与可卡因等同的现代叙事。讨论现代研究如何将可可叶用于阿片戒毒与营养保健，呼吁重新审视药物监管与文化遗产。
best_quote_en: Coca is to cocaine what potatoes are to vodka.
best_quote_zh: 可可叶与可卡因的关系如土豆与伏特加。

title: The Equity Equation
title_zh: 股权方程式
summary_en: This article distills the art of equity trade into a single, elegant formula: give up n % of your company only if the new asset raises the firm’s average outcome enough that the remaining (1‑n) share is worth more than the whole before. The author demonstrates how to apply this principle to VC deals, employee grants, and even salary‑equity conversion. By treating every transaction as a test of the 1/(1‑n) multiplier, founders can quantify the true value of a deal, avoid over‑dilution, and make data‑driven decisions. The piece blends practical examples—Y Combinator’s 7 % stake, Sequoia’s 30 % offer, and a 20 % boost from a top engineer—with a philosophical reminder that equity is a tool, not a curse. The reader emerges with a clear, actionable framework that turns intuition into measurable risk assessment, and underscores the importance of anticipating how a single percentage point can alter a startup’s trajectory.
summary_zh: 文章把股权交易简化为 1/(1‑n) 公式：只有当新增资产提升平均收益，使剩余 (1‑n) 股价值超过原公司整体时，才可放弃 n % 股权。作者举例 YC 7 %、Sequoia 30 % 与 20 % 的技术人才增值，说明如何用公式衡量融资与员工股权的价值。读者学会用数据驱动决策，避免过度稀释，并意识到每个百分点的股权分配都可能决定创业公司命运。
best_quote_en: If you trade half your company for something that more than doubles the company's average outcome, you're net ahead.
best_quote_zh: 如果你把公司的一半股份换成能让公司平均收益超过两倍的东西，你就会净赚。

title: Qualia-based emotion steering makes LLMs attribute conscious states to themselves
title_zh: 基于质感的情感引导使LLM将意识状态归因于自身
summary_en: This study probes the intersection of affective representation and self‑attribution in large language models (LLMs). By injecting emotion vectors—derived from a corpus of 171 affective states—into the residual stream of Qwen3‑32B and Qwen3‑235B, the author demonstrates that steering toward positive or negative valence can shift the models’ YES/NO logits on questions probing phenomenal experience, desire, introspection, and moral patienthood. Crucially, the effect is specific to self‑attribution queries, surviving a battery of controls: factual knowledge, self‑reference without affect, appraisal‑style emotions, random perturbations, and a regression‑to‑uncertainty model. The results suggest a causal entanglement between affective encoding and the emergent “consciousness‑adjacent” self‑model, hinting that LLMs may inhabit distinct persona‑spaces when primed with qualia. Though the statistical significance remains marginal, the work opens a pathway to interrogate whether affective steering can reliably modulate LLM self‑perception, with implications for persona design, safety, and the broader debate on machine consciousness.
summary_zh: 作者通过在 Qwen3‑32B 与 Qwen3‑235B 的残差流中注入情感向量，探讨情感表征与自我归因的关系。实验表明，正负情绪导向能显著改变模型对“有意识”“有欲望”“能感知”等自我提问的 YES/NO 预测，且此效应在多重对照实验中保持稳健。结果提示，情感编码与模型的“意识相关”自我模型在潜在空间中存在因果耦合，为人格设计与安全评估提供新思路。
best_quote_en: Qualia steering appears to move the model into a region of activation/persona space where experiential, affective, desiderative, and consciousness‑adjacent features are jointly up‑weighted.
best_quote_zh: 情感导向似乎将模型推入一个激活/人格空间，在该空间中体验、情感、欲望和与意识相近的特征被共同放大。

title: The Unprecedented Profiteering Revealed by Donald Trump’s Financial Disclosure
title_zh: 唐纳德·特朗普财务披露揭示前所未有的牟利
summary_en: The Bloomberg exposé of Donald Trump’s 2025 financial disclosure reveals an unprecedented windfall, largely from crypto token sales linked to World Liberty Financial and the memecoin $TRUMP. The piece dissects how Trump’s office was leveraged to amass $2.2 billion in earnings, with $1.4 billion tied to crypto ventures. It uncovers a tangled web of conflicts of interest, including a secret sale of a stake in World Liberty to the UAE ruling family and a partnership with Binance, a company mired in money‑laundering allegations. The article frames Trump’s profiteering as both a political and financial case study, illustrating how public office can be weaponized for personal gain. It raises critical questions about regulatory enforcement, transparency, and the ethical boundaries of presidential business. The narrative invites readers to scrutinize the intersection of politics, technology, and finance, urging vigilance against future abuses of power.
summary_zh: 本篇报道揭露特朗普在任期内通过加密资产获利逾22亿美元，凸显政务与私利交织的深层风险。
best_quote_en: The $2.2 billion in profits last year suggest that the White House has been a uniquely powerful prop for Trump’s pitches.
best_quote_zh: 去年2.2亿美元的利润表明，白宫已成为特朗普推销的独特强大支柱。

title: Retro Recomendo: Outdoor Essentials
title_zh: Retro Recomendo：户外必备品
summary_en: Retro Recomendo revives the founder’s early, evergreen gear recommendations, delivering concise, practical advice every six weeks. By curating a single theme from a decade of experience, the series turns nostalgia into a reliable resource for outdoor enthusiasts. Each entry—whether a prescription dive mask, collapsible trekking poles, or a hand‑cranked lantern—offers tangible, cost‑effective solutions that readers can immediately test. The format emphasizes user‑generated validation: comments from subscribers confirm real‑world performance, turning the list into a living community testimonial. This approach blends the intimacy of a personal blog with the authority of a curated catalog, striking a balance between expert insight and actionable steps. The recurring sponsor segment subtly reminds readers of data privacy risks, adding a timely layer of relevance. Overall, Retro Recomendo exemplifies how a simple, recurring newsletter can create enduring value by spotlighting practical, low‑cost gear that enhances outdoor experiences while fostering a sense of shared discovery.
summary_zh: Retro Recomendo 用六周一期的形式，将创始人十年积累的户外必备品精华重新整理，既保留了怀旧情怀，又提供了可落地的实用建议。每条推荐——如处方潜水镜、可折叠登山杖或手摇灯——都兼具成本效益与即时可用性，读者评论证明其真实效果。此系列将个人博客的亲和力与策划目录的权威性结合，形成了专家洞察与可执行步骤的双重价值。赞助环节提醒数据隐私风险，增加时事关联。总的来说，Retro Recomendo 展示了简易、周期性通讯如何通过聚焦低成本、提升体验的装备，持续创造价值并激发共同探索的社区感。
best_quote_en: I wish I had realized years ago that you can get scuba masks with inexpensive prescription lenses.
best_quote_zh: 我多么希望早些年就意识到，你可以用价格低廉的处方镜片买到潜水面罩。

title: Raising a Dog & Mastering Calm Assertive Energy | Cesar Millan
title_zh: 养狗与掌握冷静自信能量 | Cesar Millan
summary_en: In this episode, Cesar Millan invites listeners to reframe their own energy as the cornerstone of a calm, compliant dog. He dissects the hardwired pack hierarchy—front, middle, back—and shows how a dog’s position dictates its needs, anxieties, and learning curves. By aligning human and canine rhythms, listeners learn actionable tools: silent, confident greetings; weighted backpacks to structure walks; and early‑morning routines to curb aggression. The conversation also bridges human‑human interactions, highlighting how balance of energy underpins authority and trust. Beyond dog training, the episode offers a broader lesson: intentional energy management can improve any relationship, from pets to colleagues. The content is both a practical guide and a philosophical treatise on leadership, making it valuable for pet owners, trainers, and anyone curious about the science of behavior. The episode’s blend of expert insight, case‑study examples, and future‑focused tools makes it a compelling resource for those seeking to master calm assertive energy in any domain.
summary_zh: Cesar Millan 通过阐释犬类群体层级与人类能量平衡，展示了如何用自信、沉默的方式引导狗狗，从而缓解焦虑和攻击行为。此内容既是实用训练指南，也是关于领导与能量管理的哲学思考，适合宠物主人、训练师乃至任何想提升人际关系的人。
best_quote_en: We discuss how to determine if your dog is front, middle, or back of the pack order—a hardwired feature that determines much of their needs and personality.
best_quote_zh: 我们讨论如何确定您的狗是群体中的前位、中位还是后位——这是一种硬编码特征，决定了它们大部分需求和性格。

title: An Alternative Theory of Unions
title_zh: 关于工会的另一种理论
summary_en: The article reexamines the mid‑century union boom as an economic aberration rather than a moral triumph, drawing parallels with the rapid‑growth startup era. It argues that high union wages were a byproduct of companies overpaying for speed and reliability, much like tech firms that spent heavily on infrastructure to accelerate growth. By framing unions as “Razorfish‑like” over‑investments, the author challenges the heroic narrative that underpins modern union decline. The piece invites readers to rethink labor history through the lens of business economics, highlighting the cyclical nature of over‑investment and the role of market conditions in wage formation. It offers a provocative, data‑driven critique that urges a pragmatic reassessment of labor policies and a clearer understanding of how growth dynamics shape worker compensation.
summary_zh: 文章把20世纪中期工会繁荣视为经济异常，而非道德胜利，借科技创业快速扩张作比。作者指出高工资是公司为速度与可靠性过度投资的副产品，类似早期互联网公司对基础设施的高额支出。通过把工会比作“Razorfish”，挑战了传统英雄叙事，提醒我们用商业经济学视角重新审视劳动史，揭示过度投资与工资形成的周期性关系，促使人们以更务实的态度思考工会与劳动政策。
best_quote_en: In other words, those workers were not paid what their work was worth.
best_quote_zh: 换句话说，那些工人并未获得与他们工作价值相称的报酬。

title: A conceptor by any other name
title_zh: 另一种叫法的概念器
summary_en: This article demystifies the conceptor—a soft projection operator originally coined in reservoir computing and now repurposed for modern LLM steering. By framing conceptors as ridge‑regression shrinkage operators, the author bridges decades of signal‑processing, statistical regularization, and quantum measurement theory, offering a unified vocabulary that spans fuzzy subspaces, Wiener filters, and POVMs. The piece not only clarifies the mathematics but also provides concrete persona‑transplantation examples, illustrating how conceptors can selectively dampen or amplify latent directions to steer a model’s behavior without catastrophic interference. It invites readers to think of conceptors as the next generation of linear probes, capable of compositional Boolean operations and adaptive aperture tuning. The post encourages a systematic exploration of activation space by iteratively removing known concepts, hinting at a future where entire semantic manifolds are mapped and manipulated with elegant linear algebra. Its blend of historical context, practical guidance, and forward‑looking speculation makes it a valuable resource for researchers and practitioners seeking nuanced control over large language models.
summary_zh: 文章把概念器从储水计算迁移到 LLM 方向控制，阐释其为岭回归收缩算子，连接信号滤波、统计正则化与量子测量。通过实例展示如何用概念器精准调节潜在方向，避免干扰。作者把概念器视为下一代线性探针，支持布尔运算与自适应孔径。文中还提出逐步剥离已知概念、映射语义空间的宏观思路，预示未来对激活空间的系统性把握。兼具历史梳理、实战示例与前瞻洞见，值得从事 LLM 控制的科研与工程者阅读。
best_quote_en: Conceptor is simply the shrinkage operator of ridge-regressing onto your concept examples.
best_quote_zh: 概念器仅仅是对岭回归收缩算子在你的概念样本上的实现。

title: How Political Is This Supreme Court?
title_zh: 这最高法院有多政治？
summary_en: The piece interrogates the prevailing narrative that the U.S. Supreme Court is a purely partisan institution, arguing instead for a more nuanced understanding. It examines the 2026 term’s landmark rulings—voting‑rights erosion, executive firing powers, and the Fed exception—to illustrate how conservative justices sometimes deviate from ideological expectations. By spotlighting the four so‑called “political” justices—Roberts, Barrett, Kavanaugh, and Gorsuch—the author contends that their willingness to cross ideological lines on high‑stakes cases reflects a pragmatic, law‑first approach rather than blind partisan alignment. The article frames this as a strategic recalibration that balances institutional integrity with policy outcomes, suggesting that the Court’s decisions are shaped by a mix of legal reasoning, political calculation, and institutional concerns. It invites readers to reconsider simplistic labels and to appreciate the complex interplay of ideology, law, and politics that defines modern jurisprudence.
summary_zh: 文章挑战将最高法院视为纯粹党派机构的主流叙事，主张更细腻的理解。它剖析2026年关键判决——削弱投票权、行政人事权和联邦储备例外，展示保守派大法官有时偏离预期的案例。聚焦“政治”四位大法官——罗伯茨、巴雷特、卡瓦尼和戈萨奇，作者认为他们在高风险案件中跨党派投票体现的是务实、以法为先，而非盲目党派。文章将此视为平衡机构完整性与政策结果的战略再校准，暗示最高法院决策由法律推理、政治计算与机构考量共同塑造，呼吁读者重新评估简单标签，洞察现代司法的复杂互动。
best_quote_en: The Court is neither entirely political nor entirely apolitical. We have to be a little more nuanced in the way we go about this.
best_quote_zh: 法院既不完全政治，也不完全非政治。我们必须以更细腻的方式来对待它。

title: How Dopamine & Serotonin Shape Decisions, Motivation & Learning | Dr. Read Montague
title_zh: 多巴胺与血清素如何塑造决策、动机与学习 | Dr. Read Montague
summary_en: Dr. Read Montague’s interview on the Huberman Lab delves into the neurochemical choreography that governs motivation, learning, and decision‑making. He explains how dopamine’s reward‑prediction error signals drive goal‑oriented effort, while serotonin provides a counterbalancing tone that shapes persistence and risk tolerance. The discussion highlights how SSRIs, low‑effort high‑engagement media, and even AI‑derived models can dampen dopamine’s salience, offering a cautionary lens on modern digital habits. Montague also links dopamine dynamics to clinical conditions such as Parkinson’s, ADHD, and trauma, illustrating how aberrant signaling can alter motivation and learning trajectories. The conversation concludes with forward‑looking themes—deep‑brain stimulation, brain‑machine interfaces, and large‑language models—underscoring the growing convergence between neuroscience and AI. For readers, the episode offers actionable insights into how to modulate motivation, the pitfalls of dopamine‑driven consumption, and a roadmap for future research that bridges biology and technology.
summary_zh: 在Huberman实验室的访谈中，读蒙塔古博士深入剖析多巴胺与血清素如何共同塑造动机、学习与决策。他指出多巴胺的奖励预测误差驱动目标导向的努力，而血清素则提供对抗性调节，影响坚持与风险偏好。讨论还揭示SSRIs、低努力高参与的媒体以及AI模型如何削弱多巴胺的显著性，警示现代数字习惯的潜在危害。博士进一步将多巴胺动力学与帕金森、ADHD及创伤等临床状态关联，说明信号失衡如何改变动机与学习路径。结尾聚焦深脑刺激、脑机接口与大型语言模型等前沿议题，强调神经科学与AI的交叉融合。对读者而言，节目提供可操作的激励调节技巧、对多巴胺驱动消费的警惕，以及未来跨学科研究的路线图。
best_quote_en: Dopamine as a currency
best_quote_zh: 科学是一项接触式运动

title: Death by 1,000 Compromises: How to Tap Into Founder Mode
title_zh: 千次妥协的死亡：如何进入创始人模式
summary_en: Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, shares his personal manifesto for staying in founder mode. He traces his journey from a directionless MBA graduate to a serial entrepreneur, anchored by a yearly ritual called the Book of Life. Each year he reviews where he stands, learns from past goals, and commits to a single transformative objective—whether it’s quitting a job, launching a company, or shifting his personal habits. The practice, he argues, keeps a founder’s “North Star” alive, preventing the dilution of vision that leads to endless compromises. Pincus also warns against the “expert witness” trap—having the knowledge but no authority to act—and stresses the importance of owning control and making bold, unpopular decisions. His narrative blends practical tactics, psychological insight, and a call to “stop time” when market shifts demand a pivot. For anyone who wants to lead with conviction and avoid the paralysis of endless trade‑offs, this post offers a roadmap and a philosophical anchor.
summary_zh: 马克·平克斯在这篇文章中阐述了他保持创始人模式的秘诀——每年用《生命之书》审视自我、总结过去、设定突破性目标。通过“停止时间”的决策与“专家证人”陷阱的警示，他强调拥有决策权、敢于做出不受欢迎的大胆举措，才能防止无数妥协导致的失去方向。文章将实践与心理洞察交织，给创业者以行动指南与精神灯塔，帮助他们在快速变革中保持北极星。
best_quote_en: Know your goal or suffer death by 1,000 compromises.
best_quote_zh: 知道你的目标，否则会因千次妥协而死去。

title: The Hacker's Guide to Investors
title_zh: 黑客的投资者指南
summary_en: This essay demystifies the opaque world of venture capital for tech founders, offering a candid, actionable playbook. It exposes the paradox that investors—especially angels—are both the lifeblood of Silicon Valley hubs and the most misunderstood players, whose motivations hinge on momentum, brand, and risk appetite rather than pure technical insight. The author critiques the inflated valuation game, urging founders to prioritize speed and product over chasing high pre-money numbers. He warns of the emotional volatility and procedural inertia that can derail deals, and highlights the subtle yet powerful role of networking and reputation. By framing investors as opportunistic, risk‑averse, and heavily dependent on peer validation, the essay equips hackers with realistic expectations and strategic tactics—such as early traction, selective outreach, and disciplined fundraising timelines. Ultimately, it challenges founders to balance ambition with pragmatism, turning investor interactions into strategic assets rather than liabilities.
summary_zh: 本文揭示风投世界的隐秘与误区，指出投资者更多关注势头与品牌而非技术，强调估值泡沫与融资耗时的风险。作者用案例与经验教训，提醒创始人应以产品为先，快速获取早期用户，慎重追求高估值，并通过精准人脉与稳健融资节奏，将投资者关系转化为战略资产。
best_quote_en: Investors don’t realize how much it costs to raise money from them.
best_quote_zh: 投资者并未意识到从他们那里筹集资金的成本有多高。

title: The Pharos Gate / Felines of New York
title_zh: 《Pharos Gate / 纽约的猫咪》
summary_en: The article juxtaposes two distinct literary offerings, each revealing a different facet of contemporary book culture. Nick Bantock’s seventh installment in the Griffin & Sabine saga is celebrated for its meticulous blend of epistolary narrative and tactile artistry, offering readers a quasi‑interactive experience that blurs the line between page and personal correspondence. The reviewer underscores the author’s commitment to resolving long‑standing narrative ambiguity, thereby satisfying a devoted fan base. In contrast, Jim Tews’s photographic compendium, Felines of New York, captures the city’s diverse cat population through candid portraits and brief interviews, turning stray and domestic felines into cultural protagonists. The piece highlights the emotional resonance of animal storytelling and the subtle commentary on urban life and immigration. Together, the reviews illuminate how books can simultaneously entertain, document, and provoke reflection, reminding readers that the medium remains a living, breathing dialogue between author, subject, and audience.
summary_zh: 文章对比两部书，揭示当代书籍的多元面貌。Bantock 的《Pharos Gate》以书信与艺术交织的方式，满足读者对未解情节的渴望；Tews 的《纽约的猫咪》则通过猫咪肖像与访谈，呈现城市动物的多样性与人文情感。两篇评论提醒我们，书本既是娱乐，也是记录与对话，让读者在文字与影像中体验情感与思考。
best_quote_en: The ultimate fate of Griffin and Sabine remained tantalizingly mysterious until last month with the release of The Pharos Gate.
best_quote_zh: 对我来说，表达爱更在于我不做的事，而非我做的事。例如，如果我爱你，我不会在你的卧室门外拉屎。

title: Essentials: Using Play to Rewire & Improve Your Brain
title_zh: Essentials：通过游戏重塑与提升大脑
summary_en: Huberman’s latest Essentials episode reframes play as a deliberate, brain‑shaping tool that transcends childhood. He explains how playful activities ignite distinct neural circuits and release neuromodulators, creating low‑stakes arenas where adults can safely experiment with new roles. The discussion bridges basic neuroscience with practical advice: from dynamic movement to role‑play and chess, each form of play is mapped to specific cognitive flexibilities—creative thinking, problem‑solving, and neuroplasticity. Huberman stresses that adults can re‑introduce play intentionally, turning leisure into a structured training ground for the mind. The episode offers actionable strategies—timed “play breaks,” group improvisation, and role‑play scenarios—that readers can apply immediately. It also highlights the evolutionary logic behind play, suggesting that our brains are wired for curiosity and that play‑based learning aligns with that architecture. For anyone seeking to boost creativity, resilience, or lifelong learning, the episode provides a clear, evidence‑based playbook that blends science with everyday practice.
summary_zh: Huberman 在最新的 Essentials 章节中，将游戏重新定位为成年人可有意识地塑造大脑的工具。他阐述了游戏如何激活特定神经回路并释放神经递质，创造低风险环境，让成人在探索新角色时安全学习。内容结合基础神经科学与实用建议：从动态运动、角色扮演到棋类游戏，每种游戏形式都对应特定的认知灵活性——创造力、问题解决和神经可塑性。Huberman 强调成年人可有意地重新引入游戏，将休闲转化为结构化的思维训练。节目提供可立即应用的行动方案，如定时“游戏休息”、小组即兴表演和角色扮演情境，帮助提升创造力与韧性。它还揭示游戏的进化逻辑，说明大脑天生渴望好奇，游戏式学习正符合这一架构。
best_quote_en: Play engages specific brain circuits and neurochemicals, allowing us to explore different roles and learn in low‑stakes environments uniquely suited to learning.
best_quote_zh: 游戏激活特定的大脑回路和神经化学物质，让我们在低风险环境中探索不同角色并学习，特别适合学习。

title: Guy Oseary — The Legendary Hollywood Power Broker on 5-Minute Decisions, 36 Years of Managing Madonna, 26 IPOs, and Spotting Magic First (#874)
title_zh: Guy Oseary — 传奇好莱坞权力经纪人：5分钟决策、36年管理麦当娜、26次IPO与首创魔法（#874）
summary_en: Guy Oseary, the legendary Hollywood power broker, serves as a living case study in the art of rapid decision‑making and long‑term talent cultivation. In this episode, Tim Ferriss dissects how a single five‑minute choice can ripple across a career, citing Oseary’s 36‑year stewardship of icons like Madonna, U2, and the Red Hot Chili Peppers as proof. Beyond the music arena, the conversation turns to venture strategy—Oseary’s early bet on Vita Coco, the founding of A‑Grade Investments, and the launch of Sound Ventures, which now manages nearly $2 billion and has produced 86 exits and 26 IPOs. The dialogue blends anecdote with hard data, offering listeners a roadmap for spotting “magic first” opportunities, balancing gut instinct with analytical rigor. For entrepreneurs, investors, and creatives alike, the episode is a masterclass in speed, foresight, and the relentless pursuit of value.
summary_zh: 戈伊·奥西里以其五分钟决策与长期人才发掘的双重优势，成为好莱坞传奇。节目中，他的36年音乐管理生涯（包括麦当娜、U2等）与早期投资（维塔可、A‑Grade、Sound Ventures）交织，展示了从直觉到数据的全流程。通过案例与实战数据，听众能学到如何快速识别并抓住“先发奇迹”，并在创业与投资中实现价值最大化。
best_quote_en: Guy has been spotting talent before anyone else could see it for nearly four decades.
best_quote_zh: 戈伊·奥西里在近四十年来一直在别人看不到的地方发现人才。

title: Sali Hughes on beauty: the five makeup brushes I can’t do without
title_zh: Sali Hughes谈美妆：我不可或缺的五支化妆刷
summary_en: Sali Hughes’ feature in The Guardian turns a seemingly mundane list of brushes into a concise, actionable guide for beauty enthusiasts. By spotlighting five essential tools—each chosen for its versatility and problem‑solving power—she demonstrates how a minimal kit can address common makeup mishaps, from concealer runs to smudged eyeliner. The article’s tone balances expert insight with approachable language, making it suitable for beginners while still offering depth for seasoned users. Hughes’ emphasis on the ‘magic’ of the fluffy concealer brush underscores the importance of texture and technique, reminding readers that the right tool can simplify the application process and improve overall finish. The piece also subtly critiques the clutter often associated with beauty arsenals, advocating for a streamlined approach that prioritizes quality over quantity. In a market saturated with product reviews, this article stands out for its practical focus, clear recommendations, and the personal touch that invites readers to experiment with a pared‑down routine.
summary_zh: Sali Hughes在《卫报》上分享的五支必备化妆刷，既是实用指南，也是一种对繁杂美妆工具的简化思考。她挑选的每一支刷子都兼具多功能与问题解决能力，强调工具与技术并重，帮助读者从容应对遮瑕、眼影、粉底等常见难题。文章既适合初学者，也提供足够深度满足经验者，体现了专业见解与亲切语调的平衡。
best_quote_en: It magically smooshes everything back together.
best_quote_zh: 它能把一切魔法般地重新融合在一起。

title: Two Kinds of Judgement
title_zh: 两种判断
summary_en: The essay unpacks a subtle cognitive bias: we conflate all judgments with an evaluative intent, when in fact most are instrumental. By distinguishing between judgments whose goal is accuracy—court rulings, grades, competitions—and those whose goal is selection—college admissions, hiring, dating—the author reveals why rejection feels so personal. The key insight is that selection decisions operate in a high‑stakes, noisy environment where marginal errors rarely matter; thus, treating oneself as a passive victim inflates the emotional impact of a ‘mistake.’ The piece encourages a reframing: view the decision‑maker as a customer or a tool rather than a magistrate, thereby reducing the expectation of fairness and freeing one to act strategically. It also hints at practical tactics—selling oneself more effectively, understanding the normal distribution of applicant pools—to mitigate the psychological toll of rejection. The argument is rooted in probability theory, social psychology, and behavioral economics, offering a nuanced, data‑driven lens that blends theoretical depth with actionable advice for anyone navigating evaluative systems.
summary_zh: 作者区分了两类评判：一类追求准确性（法院判决、考试、比赛），另一类服务于选择（录取、招聘、约会）。大多数评判并非针对个人，而是基于噪声与高风险的决策场景，误判的边际影响往往微乎其微。把评判者视为客户而非法官，能降低公平预期，减少对拒绝的情绪反应。文章结合概率论与社会心理学，提供了自我推销与理解申请分布的实用策略，帮助读者在评估体系中更主动、更理性。
best_quote_en: If you regard someone judging you as a customer instead of a judge, the expectation of fairness goes away.
best_quote_zh: 如果你把评判你的人当作顾客而不是评判者，公平的期望就会消失。

title: An Inconvenient Moment for an Extreme Global Heat Wave
title_zh: 极端全球热浪的尴尬时刻
summary_en: Bill McKibben’s July 2026 dispatch is a stark, data‑rich chronicle of a planet in crisis, weaving satellite telemetry, ocean heat budgets, and political inertia into a narrative that feels both urgent and inevitable. He maps the escalating heat dome that battered Europe and the U.S., quantifies the unprecedented oceanic temperature spike, and foregrounds the paradox of dwindling weather‑balloon launches amid rising extremes. The piece is a sobering reminder that the climate system’s inertia is outpacing policy momentum; the author’s call to action is not a plea for alarmist rhetoric but a demand for evidence‑driven stewardship. McKibben also exposes the ideological calculus behind funding cuts, framing the debate as a contest of narrative rather than science. The article compels readers to reassess the link between political will and the planet’s thermal trajectory, urging a recalibration of priorities that balances economic anxieties with planetary limits. It is a critical, nuanced study that blends empirical analysis with a call for pragmatic, data‑driven governance.
summary_zh: 麦基本在2026年7月的报道以严谨的数据与政治分析，勾勒出全球热浪与海洋热量激增的严峻现实。他揭示气象仪器削减与极端天气的悖论，指出政治决策被意识形态驱动而非科学。文章警示，气候系统的惯性已超越政策节奏，呼唤以证据为导向的治理与优先重塑。
best_quote_en: The irony, as he pointed out, is that 'you’re not really changing either the visibility of the impacts or changing the trajectory of what’s actually happening.'
best_quote_zh: 讽刺的是，他指出，“你们既没有真正改变影响的可见性，也没有改变实际发生的轨迹。”

title: Essentials: The Science & Treatment of Obsessive Compulsive Disorder (OCD)
title_zh: 必备：强迫症的科学与治疗
summary_en: In this Huberman Lab Essentials episode, Dr. Andrew Huberman dissects obsessive‑compulsive disorder (OCD) from a dual perspective—neurobiology and psychology. He explains how the cortico‑striatal‑thalamo‑cortical loop fuels the repetitive “thought‑action” cycle, and shows why compulsions paradoxically reinforce obsessions instead of alleviating them. The episode rigorously reviews the evidence for exposure‑based CBT and SSRIs, comparing monotherapies with combined regimens and highlighting the modest but clinically meaningful superiority of combined treatment. A particularly striking segment details a structured protocol that teaches patients to tolerate anxiety while actively suppressing compulsive responses, effectively retraining the brain’s habit circuitry. The discussion is grounded in current imaging, genetic, and pharmacological research, and it offers a practical, evidence‑based roadmap for clinicians and patients alike, bridging theory with actionable treatment steps.
summary_zh: 这期Huberman Lab Essentials深入剖析强迫症的神经机制与心理根源，揭示冲动与恐惧循环的生物学基础，并系统评估认知行为疗法、SSRIs及其联合疗法的临床证据。作者通过案例协议展示如何在焦虑状态下训练大脑抑制强迫反应，为患者提供可操作的循证方案。
best_quote_en: Compulsive actions actually strengthen the underlying obsessions rather than relieve them.
best_quote_zh: 强迫行为实际上强化了潜在的强迫症状，而不是缓解它们。
